title: brachyglottis repanda var. arborescens
intro: Brachyglottis repanda var. arborescens, also known as the New Zealand bushman's friend, is a shrub native to New Zealand, belonging to the daisy family. It has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including relieving joint pain and sore muscles, as well as supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of Brachyglottis repanda var. arborescens include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
